title: CODEX - Poly-l-Lysine Cover-Slip Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cm8b1","text":"Poly-Lysine Coverslip Preparation ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"28u9a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d9s44","text":"This protocol describes the process of creating Poly-lysine-coated coverslips that are required for mounting tissue sections for the CODEX® experiment workflow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"57ea4","text":"Managers and supervisors - are responsible for making sure that technicians are properly trained and equipment and facility are maintained in good working order.\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a5s0r","text":"Laboratory personnel - are responsible for reading and understanding this SOP and related documents and to perform these tasks in accordance with the SOPs.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"arree","text":"Wear gloves when handling cover slips to avoid depositing epithelial cells or residues on surfaces.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqtqi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"137o3","text":"You will need a sanitized or acid-washed beaker for this procedure.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Remove 12-15 cover-slips from box. Cover-Slip brand and type are REQUIRED, not suggested. CODEX requires use of these specific cover-slips for their diagnostic process.
Gently place the desired amount of cover-slips into the beaker, spreading them out and allowing both surfaces to have contact with the solution.
Slowly swirl the beaker to spread the cover-slips around the base.
Add 7 mL of poly-lysine solution above the cover-slips to ensure that all are fully covered. 7 mL, Poly-Lysine
Cover the beaker with plastic wrap or parafilm, and secure tightly with a rubber band to prevent evaporation.
Leave cover-slips in poly-lysine solution for a minimum of 12 hours and up to one week at room temperature . 12:00:00, or up to one week.
After waiting the required incubation period, carefully remove the rubber band and parafilm from around the neck of the beaker.
Gradually pour the remaining poly-lysine solution into the proper waste container.
Fill the same beaker containing the cover-slips to half volume with double-distilled water(ddH20) or purified water.
Swirl the contents to mix the solution.
Let the beaker and cover-slips sit for 30 seconds. To prevent removal of poly-lysine, do not soak in water for >1 minute during each washing step.
Slowly pour off the water into the sink. This completes Wash #1
go to step #10
go to step #10
go to step #10
go to step #10
go to step #10
go to step #10
After completing the 7 washes, place 2 lint-free Wypall towels on the bench top.
Remove the cover-slips from the water, placing them on top of the first set of towels. Ensure the cover-slips are not overlapping to allow proper drying.
Using the specialty forceps indicated for use by CODEX, flip over each cover-slip onto the second clean towel to dry the reverse side. Specialty forceps indicated in the materials section.
Leave the cover-slips on the Wypall towel to dry.

When the cover-slips are dry, the Poly-Lysine-coated cover-slips can be stored in a sterile petri dish or similarly covered container for up to 2 (two) months. Sterile Petri Dish containing the finished Poly-l-Lysine coated cover-slips for CODEX processing.
Pour enough poly-l-lysine solution to cover the bottom of the clean beaker.
creator: Marda Jorgensen
